children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: To be eligible to receive financial assistance under this part, each State shall establish and maintain a Statewide Independent Living Council (referred to in this section as the “Council”). The Council shall not be established as an entity within a State agency.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: Members of the Council shall be appointed by the Governor or, in the case of a State that, under State law, vests authority for the administration of the activities carried out under this chapter in an entity other than the Governor (such as one or more houses of the State legislature or an independent board), the chief officer of that entity. The appointing authority shall select members after soliciting recommendations from representatives of organizations representing a broad range of individuals with disabilities and organizations interested in individuals with disabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: among its voting members, at least 1 director of a center for independent living chosen by the directors of centers for independent living within the State;

children.label: B
children.text: among its voting members, for a State in which 1 or more centers for independent living are run by, or in conjunction with, the governing bodies of American Indian tribes located on Federal or State reservations, at least 1 representative of the directors of such centers; and

children.label: C
children.text: as ex officio, nonvoting members, a representative of the designated State entity, and representatives from State agencies that provide services for individuals with disabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: other representatives from centers for independent living;

children.label: B
children.text: individuals with disabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: parents and guardians of individuals with disabilities;

children.label: D
children.text: advocates of and for individuals with disabilities;

children.label: E
children.text: representatives from private businesses;

children.label: F
children.text: representatives from organizations that provide services for individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: G
children.text: other appropriate individuals.

children.label: i
children.text: who provide statewide representation;

children.label: ii
children.text: who represent a broad range of individuals with disabilities from diverse backgrounds;

children.label: iii
children.text: who are knowledgeable about centers for independent living and independent living services; and

children.label: I
children.text: individuals with disabilities described in ; andsection 705(20)(B) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: not employed by any State agency or center for independent living.

children.label: i
children.text: individuals with disabilities described in ; andsection 705(20)(B) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: not employed by any State agency or center for independent living.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Council shall select a chairperson from among the voting membership of the Council.

children.heading: Designation by chief executive officer
children.label: B Designation by chief executive officer
children.text: In States in which the Governor does not have veto power pursuant to State law, the appointing authority described in paragraph (1) shall designate a voting member of the Council to serve as the chairperson of the Council or shall require the Council to so designate such a voting member.

children.label: i
children.text: a member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring prior to the expiration of the term for which a predecessor was appointed, shall be appointed for the remainder of such term; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the terms of service of the members initially appointed shall be (as specified by the appointing authority described in paragraph (3)) for such fewer number of years as will provide for the expiration of terms on a staggered basis.

children.heading: Number of terms
children.label: B Number of terms
children.text: No member of the Council, other than a representative described in paragraph (2)(A) if there is only one center for independent living within the State, may serve more than two consecutive full terms.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), any vacancy occurring in the membership of the Council shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment. The vacancy shall not affect the power of the remaining members to execute the duties of the Council.

children.heading: Delegation
children.label: B Delegation
children.text: The appointing authority described in paragraph (3) may delegate the authority to fill such a vacancy to the remaining voting members of the Council after making the original appointment.

children.label: A
children.text: develop the State plan as provided in ;section 796c(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: monitor, review, and evaluate the implementation of the State plan;

children.label: C
children.text: meet regularly, and ensure that such meetings of the Council are open to the public and sufficient advance notice of such meetings is provided;

children.label: D
children.text: submit to the Administrator such periodic reports as the Administrator may reasonably request, and keep such records, and afford such access to such records, as the Administrator finds necessary to verify the information in such reports; and

children.label: E
children.text: as appropriate, coordinate activities with other entities in the State that provide services similar to or complementary to independent living services, such as entities that facilitate the provision of or provide long-term community-based services and supports.

children.label: A
children.text: in order to improve services provided to individuals with disabilities, work with centers for independent living to coordinate services with public and private entities;

children.label: B
children.text: conduct resource development activities to support the activities described in this subsection or to support the provision of independent living services by centers for independent living; and

children.label: C
children.text: perform such other functions, consistent with the purpose of this part and comparable to other functions described in this subsection, as the Council determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: The Council shall not provide independent living services directly to individuals with significant disabilities or manage such services.

children.heading: Hearings and forums
children.label: d Hearings and forums
children.text: The Council is authorized to hold such hearings and forums as the Council may determine to be necessary to carry out the duties of the Council.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Council shall prepare, in conjunction with the designated State entity, a plan for the provision of such resources, including such staff and personnel, as may be necessary and sufficient to carry out the functions of the Council under this section, with funds made available under this part, and under (consistent with ), and from other public and private sources. The resource plan shall, to the maximum extent possible, rely on the use of resources in existence during the period of implementation of the plan.section 730 of this titlesection 721(a)(18) of this title

children.heading: Supervision and evaluation
children.label: 2 Supervision and evaluation
children.text: Each Council shall, consistent with State law, supervise and evaluate such staff and other personnel as may be necessary to carry out the functions of the Council under this section.

children.heading: Conflict of interest
children.label: 3 Conflict of interest
children.text: While assisting the Council in carrying out its duties, staff and other personnel shall not be assigned duties by the designated State entity or any other agency or office of the State, that would create a conflict of interest.

children.heading: Compensation and expenses
children.label: f Compensation and expenses
children.text: The Council may use available resources to reimburse members of the Council for reasonable and necessary expenses of attending Council meetings and performing Council duties (such as personal assistance services), and to pay reasonable compensation to a member of the Council, if such member is not employed or must forfeit wages from other employment, for each day the member is engaged in performing Council duties.
